title: Prediction: Micron Technology Stock Will Skyrocket to $2,000 in 1 Year
text: The Motley Fool article by Harsh Chauhan argues that Micron Technology could reach $2,000 per share within a year. The stock had already surged 162% by the article's writing date. The piece cites strong memory demand exceeding supply. According to Tom's Hardware, contract prices for dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) will increase by 58% to 63% in the current quarter. The author emphasizes that AI processors require substantial high-bandwidth memory (HBM), with each gigabyte consuming three times the wafer capacity of traditional DRAM. Counterpoint Research estimates HBM demand from custom AI processors could expand 35-fold between 2024 and 2028. Micron's adjusted earnings jumped 7.8 times year-over-year in Q2 fiscal 2026. The article projects fiscal 2027 earnings per share of $101.78. Using a 22x earnings multiple (S&P 500 average), the stock could theoretically reach approximately $2,239. The article contends that at 7.6 times forward earnings, Micron represents an attractive entry point despite significant recent gains.
